title: calamintha alpina subsp. aetnensis
intro: Calamintha alpina subsp. aetnensis, also known as Aetna calamintha, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It is a perennial herb native to Sicily, characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. Calamintha alpina subsp. aetnensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
